title: Athamanta tortuosa
taxonomy:
Athemanta tortuosa is a species of flowering plant. 
It belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. 
The genus Athamanta is part of the Apiaceae family and contains several species of flowering plants. 
Athemanta tortuosa is native to the Mediterranean region and is also found in parts of Europe and Asia. 
It is a perennial herb, typically growing in rocky and mountainous areas.
uses: Athamanta tortuosa is reportedly used in traditional medicine, specifically for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is used to treat various health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts are also used to treat certain types of infections and to aid in the healing process. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Athamanta tortuosa is used as a treatment for fever and other ailments.
